danger_signals.signal: point loss / game progression against player
danger_signals.visual_form: score counter on screen displaying opponent's advancing score
danger_signals.meaning: opponent gaining advantage; match state deteriorating for player

danger_signals.visual_form: ball sprite moving toward bottom of screen (player side)
danger_signals.meaning: immediate defensive action required; failure to return results in point loss

danger_signals.visual_form: player character positioned far from net while opponent is at net
danger_signals.meaning: vulnerable position; opponent can execute winning shots

safety_signals.visual_form: ball sprite returning to opponent's side after player racket contact
safety_signals.meaning: player maintains rally; point remains in play

safety_signals.visual_form: player character sprite positioned near net line
safety_signals.meaning: offensive advantage; ability to execute winning shots

safety_signals.visual_form: player score counter higher than opponent's on screen display
safety_signals.meaning: match state favorable; progress toward game/set victory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opponent character sprite (AI-controlled or second human player)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player positioned on bottom half of court; player 1 side in versus modes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: distinct character sprite from player; may include Mario as referee (official)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: opponent positioned on top half of court; player 2 side in versus modes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: single player character on court; AI opponent on opposite side; net dividing court vertically
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponent follows difficulty-level-dependent movement and shot selection patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: two player characters on one side; two AI opponent characters on opposite side; court layout displays four player positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: two human players cooperate against two AI opponents; coordinated court positioning required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ball sprite moving between court halves; both players animated in ready/swing poses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: real-time input controls racket position and swing timing to return incoming ball

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: score counter increments; ball resets to serve position; players return to baseline
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: serve phase begins; player regains initiative or opponent executes serve

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: no visual change on court; difficulty setting only accessible in menu
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent AI reaction speed, shot placement accuracy, and net aggression varies inversely with difficulty level

environmental_storytelling.example: rectangular court with net as central divider communicates symmetric competition structure; baseline and service box markings establish spatial rules
player characters positioned on opposite baselines indicates formal competitive setup; net presence indicates offensive/defensive posture
on-screen score counter serves as explicit world-state indicator; rising numbers communicate tension and stakes escalation
Mario character serves as official; his presence legitimizes match structure and reinforces Nintendo branding within game world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to input commands; racket swings to intercept ball; moves laterally along baseline
slow reaction time; predictable shot placement; minimal net rushing; frequent unforced errors
rapid reaction; varied shot placement; aggressive net play; optimal court positioning; reduced error rate
follows arc trajectory; bounces on court; collision detection with rackets triggers return shots
disappears from court; resets to serve position; triggers score increment and behavioral reset of all players

notes: Tennis (1984) is a foundational sports simulation with minimal narrative context. World-state communication relies entirely on mechanical/spatial visualization rather than environmental storytelling or faction systems. The game presents pure sport simulation where visual manifestation is determined by match mechanics (score, ball position, player position, serve/rally/point states). No danger/safety signals exist in a narrative sense—only game-mechanical advantage/disadvantage states. Faction distinction is purely functional (player vs. opponent) rather than ideological or visual-identity based. Mario's inclusion as referee is a character cameo without narrative function.
